title: 80th meridian east - Wikipedia
content:
### Location Details for 66°33′N 80°0′E
Based on the provided text for the 80th meridian east, the coordinates 66°33′N 80°0′E fall within the following geographical and administrative boundaries:

| Data Point | Details |
| :--- | :--- |
| **Country** | Russia |
| **Administrative Division** | Yamalo-Nenets Autonomous Okrug |
| **Latitudinal Range** | Between 69°22′N and 62°48′N |
| **Geographic Context** | Land (Russia) |

### Named Places and Administrative Divisions Nearby (Russian Segment)
The following named places and territories are located along or adjacent to the 80th meridian east in the Russian Federation:

*   **Administrative Divisions (North to South):**
    *   Krasnoyarsk Krai (Multiple segments: 80°58′N; 72°11′N to 71°8′N; 70°19′N to 69°22′N)
    *   Yamalo-Nenets Autonomous Okrug (Multiple segments: 71°8′N to 70°19′N; 69°22′N to 62°48′N)
    *   Khanty-Mansi Autonomous Okrug (Starting at 62°48′N)
    *   Tomsk Oblast (Starting at 60°40′N)
    *   Novosibirsk Oblast (Starting at 56°37′N)
    *   Altai Krai (Starting at 53°57′N)
*   **Geographic Features:**
    *   Ushakov Island (80°58′N)
    *   Kara Sea (Passing between 81°7′N and 80°50′N)
    *   Dikson Island (The meridian passes just west of this island at 80°50′N)

### Russian Settlements
Specific names of settlements located exactly at 66°33′N 80°0′E are **absent** from the provided page. The text only identifies administrative regions and large geographic features (islands and seas). The only nearby named populated place mentioned is **Dikson Island**, located further north at 80°50′N.
